payload.text: I’ll inspect the app structure first, then trace the drafting and ticket views to see how agent events are already represented before touching state or UI.

I found this is a compact Electron/React app with the drafting modal and ticket detail in `src/renderer/src/App.tsx`. Next I’m reading the shared event types and Codex normalization so the metrics can be derived from existing run events instead of adding separate counters.

{
  "name": "relay",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Local-first Codex-driven kanban desktop app.",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "electron-vite dev",
    "dev:logs": "electron-vite dev 2>&1 | tee /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "build": "tsc --noEmit && electron-vite build",
    "logs": "mkdir -p \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay\" && touch \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\" && tail -n 200 -f \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\"",
    "logs:dev": "touch /tmp/relay-dev.log && tail -n 200 -f /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "preview": "electron-vite preview",
    "test": "node tests/run-tests.mjs",

The app already persists live Codex run events as JSONL under `.relay/runs`, but the renderer only keeps the newest in-memory events. I’m going to add a small read API for those logs, derive summary metrics from the same event shape, and use a modal viewer so the ticket panel stays scannable.
